common.tagline: A game of deduction and manipulation - find the relic, unmask the liars

home.heroSubtitle: A multiplayer investigation game: each side hunts its own relic, hidden across a land of ruins. You don't know who's on your team, and clues come from the characters you meet - who can lie. It's on you to sort truth from lies.
home.feature1Text: Two cults fight over their relics. You know yours - not who else belongs to it. All the useful info flows through the other players.
home.feature2Text: The elders never lie; the players do. Any clue from a seeker may be a trap - cross-check it with an elder to unmask them.
home.feature3Text: Spot who tells you the truth: those are your hidden allies. Then claim your relic at the right moment - when your side is ready and the other isn't.
home.step1Text: Move from place to place and question the characters: each knows a piece of the truth, but only gives you fragments.
home.step2Text: A seeker passed you a clue? Verify it with an elder. A contradiction means they lied - and they're playing against you.
home.step3Text: Your side wins if it locates its relic AND the other is still wrong. If both have found it, the side that triggers loses: pick your moment.
home.finalText: Gather 4 or 6 players and start a game. One phone each - the app keeps the secrets.
home.discoverText: An old land strewn with ruins. You move from place to place to wring clues out of the characters, trade them with other players, and cross-check your way to your relic. The board shows the moves; the rest is deduction.
home.discoverBoardCaption: A land of ruins to search
home.galleryBoxText: The board, the pawns, the character and relic cards, and the companion app for 4 to 6 players.
home.galleryComponentsText: Carved pawns, an illustrated board, relic and clue cards - in the collector edition.
home.galleryExtensionText: More cults, relics and hidden roles to spice up the infiltration.

rules.title: RELIQUES
rules.tagline: A hidden-team game of investigation and lies
rules.storyBody1: An old wild land - forests, rivers, mountains, marshes - strewn with forgotten ruins. Two rival sides each seek their sacred relic, hidden in one of those ruins.
rules.storyBody2: You serve one side - but you don't know who else does. The characters you meet never lie, but only give fragments; the other players do lie. Find your allies, unmask the liars, and locate your relic before the other side.
rules.mapAlt: The board: 4 zones linked by bridge-places
rules.goalBody: Your side must locate ITS relic and claim it before the other: the first side to claim correctly wins. But a wrong claim costs you - your side is blocked for a round.
rules.turnIntro: On your turn you move from place to place - zones are linked by bridges - then act where you are:
turnOptions: Question a character there: they never lie, but only give a fragment - never the whole location.
Trade a clue you've learned with another player there - except they can lie to you.
rules.npcIntro: Scattered across the map, they NEVER lie - they're your anchors. But each knows only a fragment and gives it in pieces: go see them to gather truth.
rules.npcPublicText: A clue never points at the location directly: it rules a lead out. You need several, cross-checked, to pin down where a relic lies.
rules.npcPrivateText: A rarer clue acts as a control: if it contradicts the others, someone lied to you.

npcLevers.name: Cross-check
npcLevers.text: One clue is never enough. Combine several to narrow the possible places.

npcLevers.name: Unmask
npcLevers.text: A clue that contradicts the others betrays a liar - and so an enemy.

npcLevers.name: Claim
npcLevers.text: When your side is sure, claim: the first side to name it right wins. Wrong → your side blocked for a round.

exampleSteps: You question a character: they rule a lead out for you (“not there”).
Another player “confides” a clue. True… or are they leading you astray?
You cross-check with a character (who never lies): it contradicts the player → they lied, they're an enemy.
With the truth, you pin your ruin, spot your allies, and claim while the other side is still fumbling.
rules.deduceBody: Since players lie, never trust a single clue: cross-check it with the characters, who always tell the truth. A lie betrays itself the moment a fragment contradicts it - and then you also know who's playing against you.
rules.winBody: When your side thinks it knows, one of you claims: everyone secretly names the place of THEIR relic. Your side wins if a majority of its members are right - so make sure your ally found it too. The first side to claim correctly wins; if you are wrong, your side is blocked for a round.
recapSteps: Move and question the characters, or trade with players.
Characters tell the truth, players lie: cross-check to unmask.
Find your hidden allies as the game unfolds.
Claim when your side is ready and the other isn't. Good timing wins.
rules.appBody: Everyone plays on their own phone: the app keeps the teams secret, your private clues, and resolves the claim. The board is for movement; the app, for secrets.
rules.mapBody: The board is laid out in four zones - forest, river, mountain, marsh - linked by bridge-places (the Bridge, the Crossroads, the Ford) you travel through. Each zone holds three places: a temple, a tomb and a workshop, each marked with its icon. A place is pinned by its zone (the terrain) and its icon (the type) - that pair is what you cross-check to locate a relic. Elders stand all over, bridges included; relics only hide in the zone places.
rules.exDedIntro: You serve the Moon. From elders you know speak of YOUR relic, you gather piece by piece:
exDedClues: the Engraver: not in the Forest
the Engraver: not in the Mountains
the Augur: not in the Marsh
rules.exDedMid: → only one region of four is left: the River.
rules.exDedDone: In the River, the only one left is the Tomb → The Tomb of the Waters. Your relic is there.
rules.exLieIntro: Elders never lie; players do. The worst lie is about an affiliation (who speaks for which side):
rules.exLiePlayer: Player 3 trades you: “the Watcher speaks for the Sun relic”
rules.exLieTrap: You believe it → you read ALL the Watcher’s clues as the Sun, and chase the wrong relic.
rules.exLieAncien: later, an elder: “the Watcher speaks for the Moon”
rules.exLieCaught: Contradiction! Player 3 lied: the note strikes itself out, and you know they’re not on your side.
rules.exLieMoral: Always cross-check what a player tells you against the elders - they speak true.

reliques.onlineIntro: An investigation game: each side hunts its own relic, hidden across a land of ruins. You don't know who's on your team, and clues come from the characters you meet - who can lie. Create a room and share the code, or join your friends. One phone per player.
reliques.tutorialCaption: The rules in under a minute.
reliques.oneScreenHint: No account, no connection: gather around a single phone.
reliques.lobbyCount: {n} player(s) · an even number is needed (4 or 6)
reliques.hotseatIntro: An investigation game: each side hunts its own relic across a land of ruins. You don't know who's on your team, and clues come from the characters you meet - who can lie. Gather, cross-check, unmask, and find your relic before the other side.
reliques.passSub: Only you see your side - everyone else, look away.
reliques.ruinsLeft: {n} possible places - bind elders to your side (affiliation) to rule places out.
reliques.enemyRelicReady: Enemy relic: {n} possible ({ruin} - they may be ready!)
reliques.bindingsEmpty: None yet - an affiliation elder tells you who speaks for which side.
reliques.notebookEmpty: Go question an elder, or trade with someone.
reliques.handTitle: In hand (to give - true or false)
reliques.tradeTitle: Give a clue to {name} - true or false
reliques.tradeProposeTitle: Trade with {name} - pick your card (true or flipped)
reliques.tradeGiveBack: Give a clue back - true or false, your call.
reliques.footerYourTurn: Move, or act where you are.
reliques.footerHotseat: Move (tap a neighbouring tile), or act where you are.
reliques.claimHint: In secret. The first side to claim correctly wins.
reliques.revealHotseat: Truth: the Sun ☀ relic was at {soleil}, the Moon ☾ relic at {lune}.
reliques.affUnknownTitle: unknown affiliation - question an affiliation elder
reliques.caughtLie: {name} lied to you - an elder disproves it.
reliques.nothingMore: This elder has nothing more for you.
reliques.claimConfirmBody: Everyone must secretly name a place. Your side only wins if you are ALL right - and if the other side is right too, they win instead.
reliques.tradeHint: Give an info. Truth, or lie (it goes flipped).
reliques.claimFailedBlocked: {name}’s side is blocked for 1 round. The game continues.
reliques.claimFailedDivided: Someone on your side was right, but not the majority - rally up!
reliques.claimFailedNobody: No one on your side had the right place - keep searching.
